name: read_multiple_files
description: Read the contents of multiple files from the agent's workspace in a single call. Each file is read with the same bounds and path-escape prevention as ``read_file``. Returns a list of per-file results and an overall truncation flag.

paths.description: List of absolute or harness-relative paths to files.

max_bytes.description: Maximum number of UTF-8 bytes per file. Passed to each individual ``read_file`` call.

max_lines.description: Optional per-file line cap. Passed to each individual ``read_file`` call.

offset.description: Byte offset at which to begin reading per file. Passed to each individual ``read_file`` call.

results.description: Per-file results in the same order as ``paths``. Each entry has the same shape as ``read_file`` output: ``{content, sha256, truncated, bytes_returned, bytes_total, error}``.

truncated.description: true iff any individual file read was truncated.

error.description: Non-null when the call itself failed (e.g. empty paths list). Individual file errors are embedded in each result entry, not here.

truncation_policy.rationale: Mirrors ``read_file``'s truncation policy per-file. The overall ``truncated`` flag is true if ANY file in the batch was truncated, letting the agent decide whether to re-read specific files.
truncation_policy.behavior_when_truncated: Each file is truncated independently per the ``read_file`` policy. The top-level ``truncated`` is true if any file result has ``truncated=true``.
